Hello, could I speak to Marcos Mansour, please?
Marcos speaking. Hi Rachel, good to hear from you. How can I help?
I am calling because David asked me to pass on a message. He said he could not join our call today.
Oh, I see. Did he say why? Sorry, you are breaking up a little.
Can you hear me now? He told me he was stuck in another meeting, and he asked if we could move our call to Friday.
Friday works for me. Let me share my screen so we can check the calendar together.
You were on mute for a moment, but I can hear you now. Yes, I can see your screen clearly.
Great. I will message David and tell him we have moved the call to Friday. Thanks for letting me know.
Marcos speaking. How can I help?
Can I take a message?
He said he would call you back.
Sorry, you are breaking up. Can you hear me?
Could you share your screen, please?

Hi Marcos, this is David. I am sorry I missed your call earlier. I am calling to pass on a message from Rachel. She said she had reviewed your proposal, and she told me she was very happy with it. She also asked if you were free for a video call on Thursday. She said she would send a meeting link by email this afternoon. One more thing: she mentioned that the connection was bad during your last call, so she asked you to use headphones this time. Please call me back when you get this, or just reply to confirm Thursday. Thanks, Marcos. Talk soon.

Good morning, Marcos. Here is your call summary for today. You have two messages from yesterday. First, the supplier called while you were out. He said the new prices were ready, and he asked if you could call him back before noon. Second, Karen from the congress left a voicemail. She told me she had joined your video meeting late because her reception was bad, and she apologized. She asked if you could share the slides again. Finally, a reminder: your team said the conference call would start at three o'clock, not two. Please dial in five minutes early. That is all for now. Have a great day.
